# Questions in Livestream 88

Live Stream #88: Popcorn is at the Gates!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=347" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:47</a> | In Irenaeus' Against Heresies, what does it mean that his dialectic is prolix? | It means wordy to excess; Falk explains that patristic writers often give long, detailed presentations before critique. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:54</a> | Was Vizier Aper-el really Moses? | He rejected that identification as a weak modern theory rather than a persuasive historical match. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=854" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:14</a> | Did ancient Egyptians have messianic expectations? | Somewhat. He says Egyptians could expect a savior king to restore order in times of chaos, but this was limited and later faded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=1176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:36</a> | In Enmerkar and the Lord of Aratta, is Aratta Urartu? | He says the identification is uncertain; Aratta may be Urartu, somewhere in Elam or Iran, or even fictional. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=1307" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:47</a> | What are "shadow man" experiences? | He said there are many theories, including phantasms, psychic imprints, or higher powers, but he did not claim certainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=1833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:33</a> | Does Exodus 2:10 imply Pharaoh's daughter knew Hebrew, and should Moses' name have a Hebrew or Egyptian etymology? | He favors an Egyptian naming possibility that resonates with the Hebrew meaning, while rejecting a simple derivation from a Thutmose-style name. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=2106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:06</a> | If Genesis 31:49 is a death threat, what is 1 Samuel 20:23? | He says the shared wording changes meaning because Genesis adds "when we are apart," while 1 Samuel expresses lasting peace between David and Jonathan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=2476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:16</a> | Could Daniel 1-6 be dated early while Daniel 7-12 is late, or could the first half be an anthology compiled later? | He leans against a second-century compilation and points to Daniel's presence in the Septuagint while allowing that variant texts could have circulated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=2718" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:18</a> | Have you read Jan Assmann, and is he well regarded? | He says Assmann is brilliant and worth reading, but speculative and sometimes overgeneralizing, so he should be read carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=2962" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:22</a> | Is it heresy to claim Greek philosophers took inspiration from Egyptian philosophy? | No. He says there is ample evidence Greek thinkers learned from Egyptian religious inquiry, though Egypt had sophisticated religion more than formal philosophy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=3332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:32</a> | Why does Sentinel Apologetics call you "Daddy Falk"? | He thinks it is meant as a friendly sign of respect from the phrase "who's your daddy," though it sounds odd to him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=3488" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:08</a> | Is Kenneth Kitchen's book on Old Testament reliability good, and are there other recommendations? | He recommends Kitchen's book despite its age and also recommends Behind the Scenes of the Old Testament. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=3832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:52</a> | Is there evidence for animals, such as dogs, going to heaven? | He gives a cautious "definite maybe," saying emergent animal consciousness and souls are a serious argument but not a settled one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=4339" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:19</a> | Would we expect to find Moses' name in hieroglyphs? | Probably not, because Moses would likely not have been prominent enough in Egypt, though Falk says an Egyptian form of the name would be recognizable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=4511" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:11</a> | What was the best book you read in 2022? | He settles on Ronen Eichler's The Ark and the Cherubim as the best book he read that year. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=4939" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:19</a> | Could the Mesha Stele's 40-year oppression be literal? | He says the text is about Omri's years or rule, not simply oppression, and the numbers do not work well as a literal 40 years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=5115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:15</a> | Why would M-shay be a better Egyptian rendering of Moshe than Mu-shay? | He says M-shay has parallels in Egyptian names and avoids problems with the Egyptian w/quail chick and the Hebrew consonants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=5395" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:55</a> | What advice do you have for getting teens interested in the Bible, especially the Old Testament? | Use tactile, visual, interactive lessons tied to serious Bible teaching, and answer their questions once their interest wakes up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=5977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:37</a> | In Luke 23:43, is paradise a blessed compartment of Sheol? | He says the issue is debated, but most commentaries he has read identify paradise with heaven rather than Sheol. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=6137" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:17</a> | What are some biblical problems with American young-earth creationism? | He points to hermeneutics, compressed chronology, Genesis 1 interpretation, and ignoring ancient Near Eastern context, while noting every creation view has problems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=6520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:40</a> | Do you think the flood was real but exaggerated? | He thinks it was real and broader than a local flood, though he does not try to date it or pin it to a precise historical event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=6938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:38</a> | What are your thoughts on Randal Rauser? | He says he has limited exposure and respects Rauser as Christian, but is not a fan of his more progressive theological approach. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=7172" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:32</a> | With the carbon-dating argument retracted, is there still evidence against the Shroud of Turin? | Yes. He says the image does not fit first-century wrapping practices or normal three-dimensional body geometry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=7568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:08</a> | Which Synoptic Gospel is the most chronologically accurate? | He cautiously leans toward Luke because Luke seems more focused on sequential historical presentation, while noting he is not a Gospel specialist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=7821" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:21</a> | Did humanity originate as monotheistic, and how did other myths and religions develop? | He leans toward early monotheism becoming polytheism quickly through separate groups, divine names, and later cultural integration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=8197" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:37</a> | Were advocates of a 1250 BC Exodus wrong because Ramses the person was named after Ramses the city? | He strongly rejects that idea, saying Egyptian evidence shows the city was named after Ramesses II, not the other way around. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=8579" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:59</a> | Can you recommend Greek and Hebrew grammar books for beginner, intermediate, and advanced study? | He recommends Kelly for Hebrew, Mounce for beginning Greek, and Daniel Wallace's Greek Grammar Beyond the Basics for advanced Greek. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=8935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:55</a> | What is the evidence for a 40-year wandering? | He points to the biblical year-38 marker, a roughly 1265 BC Exodus, later Canaanite entry evidence, Hazor, Mount Ebal, and the Merneptah Stele. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=9215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:35</a> | Which church father is a good primer for getting used to patristic writing style? | He recommends starting with Philip Schaff's Ante-Nicene Fathers volume 1 and reading the early fathers sequentially. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=9512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:32</a> | What did you mean when you called Noah's Ark a sacralizing converter? | He means a ritual object that turns unclean or profane things into clean or holy ones, and he applies that temple concept to Noah's Ark. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=9633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:33</a> | What about the flood in the Epic of Gilgamesh and Atrahasis? | He says Gilgamesh has a flood narrative, but the question was unclear and Mesopotamian literature is not his specialty, so he deferred. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=9754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:34</a> | What about the precise timing recorded in the flood narrative, such as the days of rain? | After clarification, he says he would need to study those flood-day numbers further before giving a firm answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=10048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:28</a> | Can the city of Enoch in Genesis 4:17 be linked to any known ancient city? | No. He says it is pre-flood, unattested in known records, and too far outside recoverable historical reference points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=10468" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:28</a> | If the Exodus happened, why would nomadic Israelites leave so little archaeological evidence? | He answered that tent-dwelling nomads usually leave very little behind, so scarcity of traces is not surprising by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=10901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:41</a> | Could a different translation of the Mesha Stele support a literal 40 years for Omri and his son? | He still doubts a literal 40 years because Omri and Ahab's reigns do not add up that way, though he says he would need deeper parsing for the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=11530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:10</a> | What secular sources or experts can be cited for the Exodus debate? | He said the solution is to use the archaeological and textual evidence itself and not assume Christian scholars are automatically biased. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=11991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:51</a> | What are your thoughts on the Sea Peoples and their likely origin? | He identifies them as Indo-European tribes from Anatolia who moved down the coastal plain and became known to Israelites as Philistines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=12175" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:55</a> | Is the Egyptian word "emu" a homonym for rib or clay, suggesting wordplay in Eve's creation tradition? | He finds no support for the claim in the sources he checks and treats it as another weak Hans Goedicke-style hypothesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MFQf6gUtm-E?t=13138" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:58</a> | How much would it cost to forge a sword? | He says he no longer has the forge, anvil, or workspace, though he used to make knives and swords and still has some photos of past work. |
